Welcome back to Singletrack Sessions. I'm with Sophie Laron, enduro racer and coach. Sophie, good to have you. Thanks, Maya. Always happy to talk shop. Today's episode is sponsored by Strava. Strava is the home for athletes. Try Strava.com today. People think racing is about nerve. You say it's about risk. It's risk management, honestly. The crash that ends your season is almost never the scary-looking one. It's the boring risk you didn't name. Name the risk before it names you, and half the danger goes away. And you have to ride the whole run as a system, not a list of corners. The mistakes come from how one section sets up the next. When I logged a full season of my own crashes, the causes were always two turns upstream. That's almost word-forward what a friend in finance says about a portfolio. It's the same. It's the same discipline. A race line and a portfolio are both systems of correlated bits. You're managing the interactions, and the risk you can describe is the one you can design around. Thanks to Peak Design for backing the show. Peak Design builds bags and straps designed by people who actually ride. Cheers, Maya. That's it for this Singletrack Sessions. Ride safe.
